verses.text: In the beginning God made the heaven and the earth
The earth was formless and empty, and darkness covered the deep waters. And the Spirit of God was hovering over the surface of the waters.
Then God said, let there be light, and there was light.
God looked at the light and He saw that the light was good. God took the dark away from the light.
God named the light day, and he named the darkness night. There was evening, and then there was morning. This was the first day.
God said: There must be sky high up above the earth. There must be water above and under the sky.
God made the sky and then there was water above and under the sky.
God named that sky Heaven. There was evening, and then there was morning. This was the second day.
Then God said, “Let the water under the sky be gathered together so that the dry land will appear. And it happened.
God said that the name for the dry land was Earth. He said that the name for the water was sea. God saw that it was good.

Then God said, Let the earth grow grass, plants that make grain, and fruit trees. The fruit trees will make fruit with seeds in it. And each plant will make its own kind of seed. Let these plants grow on the earth. And it happened Genesis 1: 12 The earth grew grass and plants that made grain. And it grew trees that made fruit with seeds in it. Every plant made its own kind of seeds. And God saw that this was good.
It became night and then it was morning. That was the 3rd day.

God said: There must be lights in the sky. They will show when it is day and when it is night. They will be used for signs to show when special meetings begin. They will also show when it is summer, winter, spring or autumn. The lights will also show when the days and years start and when they end.
Let the lights in the sky shine and give light to the earth. Then it happened.
God made the sun, the moon and the stars. The biggest light, the sun, to shine in the day and the smaller light, the moon, had to shine at night.
God put them in the sky to give light on the earth.
He put them in the sky to rule over the day and over the night. They separated the light from the darkness. And God saw that this was good.
There was evening, and then there was morning. This was the fourth day.
Then God said, Let the water be filled with many living things, and let there be birds to fly in the sky of heaven.

God created the whales, and he also created all the living things in the sea. God created every kind of bird that flies in the air. God looked at how He had made the fish and the birds, and He saw that it was good.
God blessed them, and He said: There must be more and more fish and water animals. The water must be full of fish. And there must be lots of birds on earth.
There was evening, and then there was morning. This was the fifth day.

God said: Let the land make all kinds of animals, each having babies of the same animals. The cows, the creatures that move along the ground, and the wild animals, each according to its kind.” And it was so.
God made the different wild animals, the cows and creeping animals. Each was able to make babies after itself. God looked at how He had made the animals and He saw that it was good.

Then God said, Now let Us make people who will be like Us. They will rule over all the fish in the sea and the birds in the air. They will rule over all the large animals and all the little things that crawl on the earth.
So God created humans in his own image. He created them to be like himself. He created them male and female.

God blessed them, and He said: You must have many children and fill the earth with people. You must rule over the fish in the sea and over the birds in the sky. You must rule over all the animals on the earth.
God said, I am giving you all the grain bearing plants and all the fruit trees. These trees make fruit with seeds in it. This grain and fruit will be your food.

Every animal on earth, every bird in the air, and all the little things that crawl on the earth. I am giving all the green plants to the animals. These green plants will be their food. And all these things happened.
God looked at everything that He had made, and He saw that it was very good. It became night and then it was morning. That was the 6th day.
This is how God made heaven and earth and everything in it.
On the seventh day God finished the work he was doing, And God rested from his work.
God blessed the seventh day and made it a holy day. He made it special because on that day he rested from all the work he did while creating the world.
This is the story about the creation of the Heavens and the earth. This is what happened when the Lord God made the earth and the heavens.
There were no plants or trees in the fields on earth, because the Lord God had not given rain on earth and there were no people to care for the plants.
So water came up from the earth and spread over the ground.
Then the Lord God took dust from the ground and made a man. God breathed the breath of life into the man’s nose, and the man became a living soul, and he started to live.
Then the Lord God planted a garden in the East, the name of the place was called Eden. He put the man that he made in that garden.

The Lord God planted many kinds of trees in the ground. The trees were beautiful to look at and their fruit was good to eat. In the middle of the garden. He planted the tree of understanding what is good and what is bad.
A river flowed from Eden and watered the garden. The river then separated and became four smaller rivers.
The name of the first river was Pison. This river flowed around the entire country of Havilah, where there was gold.
12There was good gold in that land, and balm and onyx stones.
The name of the second river was Gihon. This river flowed around the whole land of Ethiopia.
The name of the third river was Hiddekel . This river flowed east of Assyria. The fourth river was the Euphrates.
The Lord God put the man in the Garden of Eden to work the soil and take care of the garden.
The Lord God said to the man: You may eat fruit from any tree in the garden.
But you must not eat from the tree that gives knowledge about good and evil. If you eat fruit from that tree, on that day you will certainly die!
Then the Lord God said, I see that it is not good for the man to be alone. I will make the helper just for him.

Then the Lord God he Lord God used dust from the ground, and made all the animals and the birds. Then He said that they must come to Adam, and he must give a name to each animal. That is how the animals got their names
The man gave names to all the cows, to all the birds in the air, and to all the wild animals. But Adam did not find someone who could help him and live with him.
So the Lord God caused Adam to sleep very deeply. While he was asleep, God took one of the ribs from Adam’s body Then he closed the Adam’s skin where the rib had been.
The Lord God used the rib from the man to make a woman. Then he brought the woman to the man.
And the Adam said, here is a person like me. She was made with bones from my bones and a body from my body. She was taken out of a man, so I will call her woman.
That is why a man leaves his father and mother and is joined to his wife. In this way two people become one.
The man and his wife were naked, but they were not ashamed.
The snake was the smartest of all the wild animals that the Lord God had made. The snake said to the woman and said, Did God really tell you that you must not eat from any tree in the garden?
The woman told the snake, no, we can eat the fruit from the trees in the garden.
But God said that we may not eat the fruit from the tree that is in the middle of the garden. God said we may not eat fruit from that tree, and we may not touch it or we will die.
But the snake said to the woman, you will not die.
God knows that when you eat the fruit from that tree you will learn about good and evil, and then you will be like God!

Then the woman could see that the tree was beautiful, and the fruit looked so good to eat. She also liked the idea that it would make her wise. She then took some of the fruit from the tree and ate it. Her husband was with her, so she gave him some of the fruit, and he ate it.
Then they started to understand everything, and they saw that they were naked. They took leaves from fig trees and sewed the leaves together to make clothes.
During the cool part of the day, the Lord God was walking in the garden. The man and the woman heard him, and they hid themselves among the trees in the garden.
The Lord God called Adam and said, where are you?
The man said, I heard you walking in the garden, and I was afraid. I was naked, so I hid.
God said to the man, who told you that you were naked? Did you eat fruit from that tree that I told you not to eat from!
Adam said: Lord, it was because of the woman You gave me. She gave me some of the fruit and I ate it.
Then the Lord God said to the woman, what have you done? She said, the snake tricked me, so I ate the fruit.

So the Lord God said to the snake, because You have done this bad thing. I make bad happen to you. It will be worse for you than for any other animal. You must crawl on your belly and eat dust all the days of your life.
I will make you and the woman enemies to each other. Your children and her children will be enemies. You will bite her child’s foot, but her child foot will crush your head.

Then God said to the woman, I will cause you to have much trouble when you are pregnant. And when you give birth to children, you will have much pain. You will want your husband very much, but he will rule over you.

Then God said Adam, I told you not to eat the fruit from the tree that will make you understand what is good and what is bad. But you did not listen to Me. You listened to your wife, and you ate the fruit. Because you did not listen to Me, I will curse the ground because of you. You will have to work hard all your life for the food the ground produces.
The ground will grow thorns and weeds for you. And you will have to eat the plants that grow wild in the fields.
You will work very long and hard to get food, and your face will be wet and full of sweat. You will work hard until the day you die, and when you die, you will become dust again.
Adam named his wife Eve. He gave her this name because Eve would be the mother of everyone who ever lived.
The Lord God used animal skins and made some clothes for Adam and his wife.
The Lord God said, look, the man has become like us to know good and evil. And now the man might take the fruit from the tree of life. If the man eats that fruit, he will live forever.
So the Lord God forced the man out of the Garden of Eden to work the ground he was made from.

God forced the man to leave the garden. Then he put Cherub angels and a sword of fire at the entrance to the garden to protect it. The sword flashed around and around, guarding the way to the tree of life.
Adam made love to his wife Eve, and she became pregnant. She gave birth to her son and named him Cain. She said, With the help of the LORD I have given birth to a male child.
Later Eve gave birth again to Cain’s brother, Abel. Abel became a shepherd, and Cain became a farmer.
After some more time, Cain brought some of food that he grew from the ground and gave it as an offering to the LORD.
Then Abel brought the first lamb born to one of his sheep, killed it, and gave the best parts of it as an offering. The LORD was pleased with Abel and his offering,
But God rejected Cain offering. Cain became very angry, and Cain became very sad.
Then the LORD said to Cain, why are you angry? Why are you very sad?
If you had done the right thing, I will accept you. But if you don’t, sin is ready to attack you. Sin desires to have you, but you must rule over it.

Cain talked with Abel his brother about what God had said. And when they were alone in the field, Cain attacked Abel his brother and killed him. Then Cain said to his brother Abel, Let’s go out in the fields.” When they were out in the fields, Cain turned on his brother and killed him.
The LORD asked Cain, where is your brother Abel? He answered, I don’t know. Am I supposed to take care of my brother?
Then the LORD said, why have you done this terrible thing? Your brother’s blood is crying out to me from the ground.
You are placed under a curse and can no longer farm the soil. It has soaked up your brother’s blood, as if it had opened its mouth to receive it when you killed him.
If you try to grow crops, the ground will not help your plants grow. You will not have a home in this land. You will wander from place to place.
And Cain said to the LORD, this punishment is too hard for me to bear.
You are forcing me to leave the land, and I will not be able to be near you or have a home! Now I must wander from place to place, and anyone I meet could kill me.
Then the Lord said to Cain, no, if anyone kills you, I will punish that person seven times as much. Then the Lord put a mark on Cain to show that no one should kill him.
And Cain went away from the LORD’s presence in the land of Nod, which is east of Eden.
Cain and his wife had a son and named him Enoch. Then Cain built a city and named it after his son, Enoch.
Enoch had a son named Irad, who was the father of Mehujael, and Mehujael had a son named Methushael, who was the father of Lamech.
Lamech had two wives, Adah and Zillah.
Adah gave birth to Jabal. Jabal was the father of people who live in tents and earn their living by keeping cattle.
His brother was Jubal; Jubal was the father of people who play the harp and flute.
Zillah gave birth to Tubalcain, was the father of people who work with bronze and iron. The sister of Tubalcain was named Naamah.
Lamech said to his wives, Adah and Zillah, listen to me: A man hurt me, so I killed him. I also killed a young man for hitting me.
If seven lives are taken to pay for killing Cain, 77 will be taken if anyone killing Lamech
Adam and Eve made love again and had another son. Eve said, God has given me a son to replace Abel, whom Cain killed. So she named him Seth.
Seth had a son whom he named Enos. At that time people began to pray to the Lord.
The number of people on earth continued to increase. When these people had daughters.
Some of the angels saw that these daughters were beautiful, and they married the daughters that they wanted.
Then the Lord said, People are only human. I will not let my Spirit be troubled by them forever. I will let them live only 120 years.
In that time there were giants on earth. The giants were the children of the angels who had married the people’s daughters. The giants were very strong soldiers, and everyone knew about them.
The Lord saw that the people on the earth were very evil. He saw that they thought only about evil things all the time.
The Lord was sorry that he had made people on the earth. It made him very sad in his heart.

So the Lord said, I will destroy all the people I created on the earth. I will destroy every person and every animal and everything that crawls on the earth. And I will destroy all the birds in the air, because I am sorry that I have made them.
But Noah pleased the Lord.
This is the history of Noah’s family. He was the only man who was good and righteous. He listened to God and followed God.
Noah had three sons: Shem, Ham, and Japheth.
When God looked at the earth, God saw that the people on earth were bad, and violence was everywhere
God saw that people had destroyed the earth. and all the people had destroyed their life on earth.
God said to Noah, I will destroy all living things from the earth. Everyone has filled the earth with anger and violence. I will destroy them with the earth.
God told Noah, use gopher wood to build an ark. You must build rooms in the ark and put tar over the wood on the inside and the outside
This is the size I want you to make the boat: 300 cubits long, 50 cubits wide, and 30 cubits high.

Build a roof over the ark. Make openings like windows of 1 cubit between the roof and the sides. You must put a door on one side and make 3 floors on top of each other, a bottom floor, a middle floor and a top floor.
Understand what I am telling you. I will bring a great flood of water on the earth. I will destroy all living things that live under heaven. Everything on the earth will die.
I will make a special agreement with you. You, your wife, your sons, and their wives will all go into the boat.
Also, you will take two of every living thing on the earth with you into the boat. Take a male and female of every kind of animal so that they might survive with you.
Two of every kind of bird, animal, and creeping thing will come to you so that you might keep them alive.
Also bring every kind of food into the boat, for you and for the animals.
Noah did everything God commanded him.

7.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Then the Lord said to Noah: Go into the ark, you and all your family. I have seen that you are a good man among the people of this time.
Take with you seven pairs of every kind of clean animal, the male and the female; and one pair of every kind of animal that is not clean, the male and the female;
and also seven pairs of every kind of bird, male and female. Do this to keep them alive on the earth.
Seven days from now I will send rain on the earth. It will rain for forty days and forty nights. I will wipe from the earth every living thing that I have made.
And Noah did everything the Lord commanded him.
Noah was six hundred years old when the flood waters came on the earth.
Noah went into the ark with his sons, his wife, and his sons' wives, to escape the flood waters.
The clean animals and the animals that are not clean, the birds, and everything that moves on the ground,
came to Noah and went into the ark, two by two, male and female, just as God had commanded Noah.
After seven days the flood waters came on the earth.
It was the six hundredth year of Noah's life, on the seventeenth day of the second month. On that day all the underground waters burst open, and the windows of the sky were opened.
Rain fell on the earth for forty days and forty nights.
On that same day Noah went into the ark with his wife, his three sons — Shem, Ham, and Japheth — and the wives of his sons.
They had with them every kind of wild animal, every kind of farm animal, every kind of creature that moves on the ground, and every kind of bird.
They came to Noah and entered the ark, two by two, every living thing that breathes.
The animals going in were male and female of every living thing, just as God had commanded Noah. Then the Lord shut him in.
The flood continued for forty days on the earth. The waters rose and lifted the ark high above the ground.
The waters rose higher and higher, and the ark floated on top of the water.
The waters rose so high that they covered even the highest mountains everywhere under the sky.
The water rose more than twenty feet above the mountains, covering them completely.
Every living thing on the earth died — the birds, the farm animals, the wild animals, every creature, and all the people.
Everything on dry land that had the breath of life in it died.
God wiped out every living thing on the earth — people, animals, creatures that move on the ground, and birds. Only Noah was left, and those who were with him in the ark.
And the waters covered the earth for a hundred and fifty days.
God blessed Noah and his sons. God said to them Have many children. Fill the earth again with your people.
Every animal on earth, every bird in the air, every animal that crawls on the ground, and every fish in the sea will be afraid of you. All of them will be under your control
In the past, I gave you the green plants to eat. Now every animal will also be food for you. I give you everything on earth—it is yours
But you must not eat meat that still has blood in it. Blood is like the life of someone.
People must not kill other people, and animals must not kill people. If an animal kills a man, you must kill that animal. If a man kills another man, you must kill that man.
I have made people to be like Me, like God. If a person kills someone, he must also die.
Noah, you must have many children and their children must have more children. The earth must be full of people.
God said to Noah and his sons:
I, God, now make my promise to you and to your people who will live after you
I am also making this promise with all the birds and the animals that were in the ark with you.
This is my promise to you: All life on the earth was destroyed by the flood. But that will never happen again. A flood will never again destroy all life on the earth.
God said to Noah: I will give you a sign to show that I have made a promise with you and with all the people and animals. I will remember this forever.
I have put my rainbow in the sky. The rainbow is a proof of this promise between me, God, and the earth.

When I bring clouds over the earth, you will see the rainbow in the clouds. Genesis 9: 15 When I see this rainbow, I will remember the promise between me and you and every living thing on the earth. This rainbow says that a flood will never again destroy all life on the earth.
When I look and see the rainbow in the clouds, I will remember the promise that will continues forever. I will remember the agreement between me and every living thing on the earth.
So God said to Noah, This rainbow is proof of the Promise that I made with all living things on earth.
Noah’s sons came out of the boat with him. Their names were Shem, Ham, and Japheth. (Ham was the father of Canaan.)
These three men were Noah’s sons. And all the people on earth came from these three sons.
Noah became a farmer and planted a vineyard.
One day he drank too much of the wine that he had made, and he became drunk. He lay in his tent and started to sleep. He was also naked
Ham, the father of Canaan, saw that his father was naked and told his brothers who were outside the tent.
Shem and Japheth took a robe, put it across their shoulders, and walked backward into the tent. Then they covered their father without looking at him.
So Noah awoke from his wine and knew what his younger son had done to him.
Noah said, May there be a curse on Canaan May he be a servant to his brothers.
Noah also said, Praise the Lord, the God of Shem. Canaan will be Shem’s servant.
I pray that God will give Japheth more and more fields and land. Japheth will live in Shem’s tents. Canaan will be Japheth’s servant.
After the flood Noah lived 350 years.
Noah lived a total of 950 years; then he died.
The Lord said to Abram: ‘You must leave your land. Go away from it and from your family and the house of your father. I will show you where you must go.
I will build a great nation from you. I will bless you and make your name famous. People will use your name to bless other people.
I will bless those who bless you, and I will curse those who curse you. I will use you to bless all the people on earth
So Abram left Haran just like the Lord said, and Lot went with him. Abram was 75 years old when he left Haran.
Abram took his wife Sarai, his nephew Lot, all the slaves, and all the other things he had gotten in Haran. Then he and his group moved to the land of Canaan.
Abram walked from one side of the land to the other side. At that time the Canaanite people lived in the land. Abram went to the town of Shechem, to the big tree of Moreh.
The Lord appeared to Abram and the Lord said to him: I will give this land to your children and your family. Then Abram made an altar for the Lord because the Lord had appeared to him.
Then Abram left Shechem and traveled to the mountains east of Bethel. Abram set up his tent there. Bethel was to the west, and Ai was to the east. Abram built an altar there to worship the Lord.
Later, Abram took his tents and he went away to the south of Canaan.
During this time there was not enough food in the land of Canaan and Abram went to the land of Egypt to stay there, because there was very little food in his own land.
When they came near Egypt, Abram said to his wife Sarai: I know you are a beautiful woman.
When the Egyptian people see you, Sari, they will kill me and take you because I am your husband, but they will not kill you.

Sarai must tell the Egyptians that you are my sister, and then they will not kill me. They will think I am your brother, and they will be good to me. If you do this, you will help me and save my life.’
When Abram and Sarai came into the land of Egypt, the people of Egypt saw how beautiful Sarai was.
The people who worked for Pharaoh told him about Sarai and they took Sarai and said she must stay in the palace of the Pharaoh.
Pharaoh was good to Abram because he thought that Sarai was his sister. Pharaoh gave Abram sheep, cattle, donkeys, slaves and camels.
But the Lord made Pharaoh and his family very sick because he had taken Sarai was Abram’s wife
Then Pharaoh called Abram, and he asked Abram: Why have you done this bad thing to me? Why didn’t you tell me that Sarai is your wife?
Why did you lie to me and say Sarai is your sister? Because you lied to me, I took her as my wife and God has made us sick. Now you must take Sarai and go away. She is your wife.
Pharaoh told his servants to take Abram and his wife and all the things that belonged to him out of the land of Egypt.
Abram and Sarai took all the sheep and the things that they had and they went from the land of Egypt to the south of the land of Canaan. Lot went with them.
Abram was very rich. He had a lot of cattle, sheep and goats and Abram also had a lot of silver and gold.
Abram continued traveling around. Abram went back to Bethel. He went to the place between the city of Bethel and Hai where he and his family had camped before
The first time that Abram had been there, he had built an altar. Now he prayed to the Lord at the altar.
Lot was with Abram and Lot also had sheep, goats, cattle and tents.
There was not enough grass and food for all Abram and Lots animals, because they had too many animals, so they could not stay together.
Then the shepherds of Abram and the shepherds of Lot started to fight. At that time the people of the land of Canaan and Perizz were still living in the land.
Abram said to Lot: ‘We are family. We must not fight and the people who work for us must not fight with each other.
This land is big enough. It will be better if we separate. If you go to the left, then I will go to the right. If you go to the right, then I will go to the left. Lot, you can choose.

Lot looked and saw the whole Jordan Valley. Lot saw that there was much water there. (This was before the Lord destroyed Sodom and Gomorrah. At that time the Jordan Valley all the way to Zoar was like the Lord’s Garden. This was good land, like the land of Egypt.)
So Lot chose to live in the Jordan Valley. The two men separated, and Lot began traveling east.
Abram stayed in the land of Canaan but Lot took his sheep near to the towns of the valley and he put up his tents near the city of Sodom.
In Sodom the people did very bad things and they sinned a lot against the Lord.
After Abram and Lot had separated, the Lord said to Abram: You must look around you, to the north, to the south, to the east and to the west.
I will give you and your family all the land that you can see. This land will be your land and your family’s land forever.
I will give you lots of family, so many that no one will know how many there are. There will be as many as the grains of sand on earth.
You must now walk through the whole land and look at it because I am going to give this land to you.
Then Abram took his tents and he went to stay near the big trees of Mamre near the city of Hebron, where he built an altar to worship the Lord.
The Lord came to Abraham. Abraham was sitting in front of the door of his tent. This was near the trees of Mamre. It was very hot.
Abraham looked and suddenly he saw 3 men standing not far from him. Abraham ran from his tent to meet them and bowed down with his face to the ground.
Abraham said: My Lord, if I have found grace in your eyes please do not go past my tent and go away. Please stay for a short while.
I will bring you some water, and then you can wash your feet. You can rest here under this tree.
I will also bring you some bread and when you have eaten enough, you can go on. I am happy that you have come to me, your servant. Then the men said to Abraham: Yes, you can do what you have said.
Abraham quickly went to Sarah in the tent and told her: prepare enough flour for three loaves of bread.
Then Abraham ran to his cattle and he chose the best young calf. Abraham gave the calf to his servant and told him to kill it and cook it.
Abraham brought some cream and milk and the meat of the calf and Abraham gave it to the 3 men. They ate and Abraham stood with them under the tree.
Then the 3 men they asked Abraham: Where is your wife, Sarah? Abraham answered and said: She is here in the tent.
Then One of the men said to Abraham: I will come back next year and then Sarah will have a baby boy. Sarah was in the tent and she heard what He said.
Abraham and Sarah were very old. Sarah was too old to have a baby.
Sarah laughed and said to herself: I am too old. I can not sleep with my husband and have a baby. He is also too old.
The Man was the Lord Himself. He asked Abraham: Why did Sarah laugh? She thinks that she is too old to have a baby.
I am the Lord. I can do anything that I want to do. I will come back next year at the time that I have said, and then Sarah will have a son.
Sarah was afraid. Sarah lied and said: I did not laugh. But the Lord said: Yes, you did.
The 3 men wanted to go. They looked at the city of Sodom and Abraham walked with them to send them on their way.
The Lord said to Himself: Shall I, the Lord keep secret from Abraham about what I, the Lord am going to do.
Abraham will become a great and powerful nation, and all the nations on earth will be blessed because of him.

I chose Abraham, and he will tell his children and his descendants to live as God want them to live, to live in the right way and not to do what is wrong, and then I, the Lord will give him everything that I, the Lord has promised him.
The Lord said: Abraham, I, the Lord know that the people of Sodom and Gomorrah are doing very bad things and that they sin a lot.
I, God, am going to the cities to see if it is true. I want to know if those people are so bad.
Then the other 2 of the men started to walk to the city of Sodom. But the Lord did not go but stayed with Abraham.
Abraham asked God: Lord, when You punish those bad people, are You, the Lord also going to punish the people who are good?
If there are 50 good people in the city, will You punish them with the bad people, or will You, the Lord save the city because there are 50 good people in it?

I am sure that You will not kill the good people with the bad people. You will not punish the good people and the bad people in the same way. I know that You, God, are not like that. You, The Lord, are the Judge of the world and You always do what is right.
The Lord said: If I, God, find 50 good people in the city of Sodom, I, God will not punish the people of the city. I, the Lord will save the city.
Then Abraham said, compared to you, Lord, I am only dust and ashes. But let me bother you again and ask you this question.

Abraham said, maybe there are only 45 good people in the city of Sodom. Will You then punish all the people in the city? God answered Abraham: If I find 45 good people in the city, I, the Lord will not punish them and I, the Lord will not destroy the city.
Abraham said again: Maybe there are only 40 good people. And the Lord said: If there are 40 good people, I, the Lord will not destroy the city.
Abraham asked: Lord, please do not be angry with me. What will You do if there are 30 good people in the city? The Lord said: If I find 30 good people in the city, I, the Lord will not destroy it.

Then Abraham said: Please forgive me if I keep on asking You. Lord, what will You do if You find 20 good people there? The Lord answered: If there are 20 good people, I, the Lord will not destroy the city.

Then Abraham said: Lord, please don’t be angry with me. I will ask You only one more time. If You find 10 good people there, what will You do? And the Lord said to Abraham: If I find 10 good people there, I, the Lord will not destroy the city.
After the Lord had talked to Abraham, The LORD went away, and Abraham went to his home.
Jacob went on and there were angels of God who came to Jacob. Genises
When Jacob saw the angels, Jacob said: This is a camp of God’s soldiers’ and he named that place Mahanaim. Genises
Jacob sent a message to his brother Esau. Esau was in the land of Seir, the country of Edom. Genises
Jacob told the men to tell his brother Esau: Sir, I am your servant Jacob and I have lived with Laban all these years. Genises
Now I have oxen, donkeys, sheep, goats, slaves and slave women. I am sending you a message now. I want to know if you will be friendly and accept me back. Genises
When the messengers came back to Jacob, they said to Jacob. We went to your brother Esau. Esau is coming to meet you and He has 400 men with him. Genises
Jacob became afraid and very worried. Jacob divided all the people who were with him into 2 groups, Jacob also divided all the sheep, goats, cattle and camels. Genises
Jacob said: If Esau attacks one group, then the other group can flee. Genises
Jacob prayed to God and said: God of my grandfather Abraham, God of my father Isaac, Lord, You told me to go back to my land and to my family and You said that You would be good to me. Genises

I am not worthy but You were very good to me and You have done everything that You promised me. The first time I traveled across the Jordan River, I owned nothing but my walking stick. But now I own enough things to have two full groups. Genises
Lord, please protect me from my brother Esau, because I am afraid of him. I am afraid that he will attack me and the women and children. Genises

Lord, You have promised and said to me: I will be good to Jacob. I will make your descendants as many as the grains of sand of the sea. There will be so many that no one will be able to count them. Genises
Jacob slept there that night. Jacob took some of his animals and he sent a gift to his brother Esau. Genises
Jaco gift 200 was female goats and 20 male goats, 200 female sheep and 20 male sheep. Genises
30 nursing camel and their calves, 40 cows and 10 bulls, and 20 donkey and 10 young donkey. Genises
Jacob gave the animals to his servants. Jacob gave a flock of animals to each of them and he told them: Walk before me, but keep each flock of animals separate. Genises
Jacob told his first servant: When my brother Esau comes to you and he asks you whose servant you are, where you are going and whose animals these are, Genises
you must tell him: Sir, they belong to your servant Jacob. They are a gift that Jacob sends to you, Esau. Jacob is also coming. Jacob is behind us. Genises
Jacob said the same to the 2nd servant and the 3rd servant and to all the other servants who followed the flocks of animals. Jacon told them what they must say to Esau when they came to him. Genises

They also had to say to Esau: Your servant Jacob is also coming. Jacob is behind us. Jacob said to himself: I am sending these gifts for Esau to make him friendly. When I come to him, maybe he will be friendly to me. Genises
Jacob first sent the gifts to Esau and Jacob that night stayed in the camp. Genises
Jacob got up that night. Jacob took his 2 wives, his 2 servant women and his 11 sons, and they went through the Jabbok River. Genises
Jacob sent them through the river and then Jacob also sent his animals through the river. Genises
Jacob stayed behind, and he was alone. A Man came to him and wrestled with him until just before the sun came up the next day. Genises
When the Man saw that He could not defeat Jacob, he touched Jacob’s leg and put it out of joint. Genises
The Man said to Jacob: Let Me go because the sun will be coming up and it is almost day. But Jacob said to Him: You must first bless me, then I will let You go. Genises
The Man asked Jacob: What is your name? And he said: Jacob.
The Man said: Your name will no longer be Jacob. From now on you will be Israel. The name Israel means: You have wrestled with God and with people and you have won’. Genises
Jacob asked: Please tell me your name. And the Man said: ‘Why do you ask what my name is? The Man blessed Jacob there. Genises
Jacob named that place Peniel. Jacob said: At this place, I saw God face to face, but my life was spared. Genises
The sun came up when Jacob went away from Peniel. Jacob could not walk well because his hip was hurt. Genises
That is why the Israelites never eat the meat from the muscle in the hip bone of an animal, because the Man touched Jacob’s hip.
Jacob lived in the land of Canaan where his father Isaac had been a stranger before. Genises

Here is the story of Jacob’s family. When Joseph was young, only 17 years old, he looked after his father’s sheep and goats with his brothers, the sons of his father’s wives Bilhah and Zilpah. Joseph told his father about the bad things that his brothers did. Genises
Jacob’s other name was Israel. Jacob loved Joseph more than all of his other sons because Joseph was born when Jacob was old. Jacob gave Joseph a special coat, that had many colors Genises
Joseph’s brothers saw that their father, Jacob loved Joseph more than them and they hated Joseph. Joseph’s brothers did not want to say anything good about him. Genises
One night Joseph dreamed and Joseph told his brothers what he had dreamed. Then they hated him even more. Genises
Joseph told his brothers: I dreamed a dream Genises
Joseph said were tying bundles of wheat on the wheat field. I dreamed that my bundle of wheat was standing upright and your bundles of wheat stood around my bundle and bowed before mine. Genises
Joseph’s brothers said to him: Do you really think that you will be king and rule over us? Joseph brothers hated Joseph even more after he told them about the dream that he had had. Genises
Joseph dreamed again and Joseph told his brothers what he had dreamed. Joseph said: I dreamed again. I dreamed that the sun and the moon and 11 stars came and bowed before me. Genises

Joseph told his father and his brothers what he had dreamed and his father was angry and rebuked Joseph and Jacob said to Joseph: Don’t tell us about dreams like that. It is not right that your mother and your brothers and I should come and bow down before you. Genises
Joseph’s brothers were jealous of him, but his father thought a lot about these dreams Genises
Joseph’s brothers had to look after their father’s sheep and goats near the town of Shechem. Genises
Jacob said to Joseph: Your brothers are looking after the sheep and goats near the town of Shechem. You must go to them. Joseph said: Yes, Father, I will go. Genises

Jacob told Joseph: Go and see if everything is well with your brothers and with the sheep and goats. Go and look and come and tell me everything. So Jacob sent Joseph from the Hebron Valley and he went near the town of Shechem. Genises
Joseph was walking in the field when a man saw Joseph. The man asked Joseph: What are you looking for? Genises
Joseph said to him: I am looking for my brothers. Do you know where they are with their sheep and goats? If you know, please tell me. Genises
The man said: They are no longer here. They went away from here. They said that they were going to Dothan. Joseph followed his brothers and he found them in Dothan. Genises
While Joseph was still far from them, the brothers saw him coming. Before Joseph came to them, they made a plan to kill Joseph Genises
Joseph’s brothers said to each other: Look, here comes the big dreamer. Genises
Joseph’s brothers said Let us kill Joseph and throw him into a pit. We can tell our father that a wild animal killed Joseph. Then we will show that all of his dreams meant nothing. Genises
When Joseph’s brothers told Reuben about it, Reuben wanted to save Joseph. Reuben said: No, we must not kill Joseph. Genises

And Reuben said unto Joseph’s brothers, Shed no blood, but cast him into this pit that is in the wilderness, and lay no hand upon him; that he might save Joseph out of their hands, to deliver him to his father again. Genises
When Joseph came to his brothers, they took off his coat of many colors that Joseph was wearing. Genises
Joseph’s brothers took Joseph and threw him into an empty well that was dry. Genises

Joseph’s brothers sat down to eat, then Joseph’ brothers looked, and saw a group of Ishmaelites coming from Gilead. Their camels were carrying many different spices , balm and myrrh . The Ishmaelites wanted to sell their spices in Egypt. Genises
Judah said to his brothers: We will have nothing if we kill our brother Joseph and keep quiet about it. Genises
Let us sell Joseph to the Ishmaelites. We must not kill him. Remember, Joseph is our brother. Judah’s brothers listened to him. Genises

And when the merchants of Midian came near them, Joseph’s brothers took him out of the empty well and Joseph’s brothers sold Joseph to the Ishmaelites for 20 pieces if silver. The merchants took Joseph to Egypt. Genises
When Reuben came back to the empty well and saw that Joseph was not in the well, he was very sad, and he tore his clothes. Genises
Reuben went back to his brothers and asked them: The child is no longer there. where shall I go? Genises
Joseph’s brothers killed a goat and took Joseph’s coat and put blood on it. Genises
Joseph brothers sent Joseph’s coat of many colors to their father and told him: We found this coat. See if it is your son Joseph’s coat. Genises
Jacob looked at the coat and said: Yes, this is my son’s coat. A wild animal killed and ate him. My son Joseph has been eaten by a wild animal. Genises
Jacob was very sad. Jacon tore his clothes and put on sack-cloth. Jacob cried and mourned for Joseph for a long time. Genises
All Jacob’s sons and daughters came to comfort him, but Jacob did not want them to comfort him. Jacob said: I will continue to mourn for Joseph until I die. Jacob cried a lot about Joseph. Genises
The Midianites sold Joseph in Egypt to Potiphar. Potiphar. Potipher was an officer of the king of Egypt and the captain of Pharaih’s palace guards.
People told Jacob that there was wheat in Egypt and Jacob asked his sons: Why are you sitting and just looking at each other?
The people told me that there is wheat in Egypt. My sons, you must go there and buy wheat for us so that we can have food to eat and not die from hunger.
Then 10 of Joseph’s brothers went to Egypt to buy corn.
Jacob did not send Benjamin. (Benjamin was Joseph’s only full brother.) Jacob was afraid that something bad might happen to Benjamin.
So Israel’s (also called Jacob) sons went with other people of Canaan to buy corn in Egypt, because there was a famine in the land of Canaan.
Joseph was the ruler of the land of Egypt, and he sold the corn to the people. When Joseph’s brothers came to Joseph, his brethren bowed before him.

Joseph saw his brethren and he knew that they were his brothers, Joseph pretended that he did not know them. Joseph looked angry when he talked to his brethren and asked them: Where do you come from? Joseph Brethren said: We come from Canaan. We have come to buy food.
Joseph knew that they were his brothers but they did not know that he was Joseph.
Joseph then thought about the dream that he had had about them and he said to them: You are spies. You have come to see how you can attack our land.
Joseph brethren said to Joseph: No, we have come to buy food.
We are all sons from one father. We are honest. We are not spies.
But Joseph said to his brethren: No, you have come to see how you can attack our land.
Joseph Brothers said: We were 12 brothers, the sons of one man in the land of Canaan. Our youngest brother is still with our father, and our other brother is dead.
Joseph said to them: It is like I told you. You are spies.
Joseph said, I will test you and see if what you are saying is true. You will not leave here until your youngest brother also comes here, as sure as Pharaoh lives.
One of you must go and get your brother, but the others will stay here in prison. Then I will find out if you are telling the truth or not. You are spies, as sure as Pharaoh lives
Joseph put all his brothers in prison for 3 days.
On the 3rd day Joseph said to them: ‘I fear God. If you do what I say, I will not kill you.
If you are honest men, one of you must stay here in prison. The other brothers can go and take wheat to your hungry people at home.
Joseph said, You must then bring your youngest brother to me. Then I will believe you, and you will not die. The brothers said: Yes, we will do what you say

Joseph brothers said to each other: We are guilty because we did something very bad to our brother. We saw that Joseph was afraid and Joseph begged us to help him, but we did not listen to him. That is why we have these problems
Reuben said to his brethren: I told you not to do it to the child, but you did not want to listen. It is our fault that he is dead.
Joseph brothers did not realize that Joseph understood everything that they said to each other, because when they talked to Joseph, someone interpreted it for him.
Joseph went out and cried. Then Joseph came back and talked to his brother. Then Joseph tied up Simeon in front of his brethren.

Joseph told the servants to fill the bags with grain. Joseph brothers had given money for the corn, but Joseph did not keep the money. Joseph had their money put into their bags of corn. Then Joseph gave them what they would need for their trip back home.
Joseph’s brothers put the corn on their donkeys and they went back home.
And at the lodging place, as one of Jospeh brothern opened his sack to feed his donkey, he saw his money in the opening of his sack.
He said to his brothers: They have put my money back. Here it is in my bag. The brothers became afraid. They asked each other: What has God done to us?
Joseph brothers went back to their father Jacob in the land of Canaan. Joseph brethern told Jacob about everything that had happened.
Joseph brothen said: The man who is the ruler of the land was angry when he talked to us and he put us in prison. He said that we were spies in his land.
We told him that we were honest, and we were not spies.
We told him that we were 12 brothers, all sons of the same father, and that one brother was dead and the youngest brother was with our father in Canaan.
The ruler of the land said to us: I will find out if you are honest. One of your brothers must stay here with me. Go back and take food for your hungry people at home.
But you must bring your youngest brother to me, and then I will know that you are not spies but honest people. Then I will give your brother back and you may go to any place in our land.
Jacob’s sons opened their bags to take out the food, and each brother saw that his money bag with his money was in his bag. Then the brothers and their father all became afraid and they trembled.
Their father Jacob said to them: You want to take away all of my children. Joseph is no more, and Simeon is no more, and you would take Benjamin from me. All these things are working against me
Reuben said to his father Jacob: Father, you may kill my 2 sons if I do not bring Benjamin back to you.Father Trust me. I will bring him back to you.

Jacob said: My son Benjamin will not go with you. Benjamin brother is dead and he is the only one left. If something bad happens to him on the way, I will be so sad that I will die because I am an old man.
The famine was very bad in that country.
The people ate all the grain they had brought from Egypt. When that grain was gone, Jacob said to his sons, “Go to Egypt and buy some more grain for us to eat.”

But Judah said to Jacob, “But the governor of that country warned us. He said, ‘If you don’t bring your brother back to me, I will refuse to talk to you.’ Genesis 43: 4 If you send Benjamin with us, we will go down and buy grain Genesis 43: 5 But if you refuse to send Benjamin, we will not go. The man warned us to not come back without him.
Israel said, “Why did you tell him you had another brother? Why did you do such a bad thing to me?”

The brothers answered, “He asked lots of questions. He wanted to know all about us and about our family. He asked us, ‘Is your father still alive? Do you have another brother at home?’ We only answered his questions. We didn’t know he would ask us to bring our brother to him!
Then Judah said to his father Israel, “Let Benjamin go with me. I will take care of him. We have to go to Egypt to get food. If we don’t go, we will all die, including our children.
I will make sure he is safe. I will be responsible for him. If I don’t bring him back to you, you can blame me forever.
If you had let us go before, we could have already made two trips for food.”

Then their father Israel said, “If it is really true, take Benjamin with you. But take some gifts to the governor. Take some of the things we have been able to gather in our land. Take him some honey, pistachio nuts, almonds, spices, and myrrh.
Take twice as much money with you this time. Take the money that was given back to you after you paid last time. Maybe the governor made a mistake.
Take Benjamin and go back to the man.
I pray that God All-Powerful will help
Joseph’s brothers took the gifts and more money than they had the first time, and they took Benjamin with them. They went to Egypt, and they stood before Joseph.
When Joseph saw that Benjamin was with them, he told the manager of his house: ‘Bring these men to my house. Kill a cow and prepare food. They will eat with me at noon today
The man did what Joseph said, and he took Joseph’s brothers to Joseph’s house.

The brothers were afraid when the man took them to Joseph’s house. Joseph’s brothers said: It is because of the money that was in our bags the first time we came. They are bringing us here to catch us and put us in prison. We will be their slaves, and they will take our donkeys.
Joseph’s brethren went to talk to the man who was the manager of Joseph’s house.
Joseph’s brethren said, Please, sir, we came here before to buy food.

When we came to the place where we wanted to sleep, we opened our bags and then we saw that each man’s money was still in his bag. It was the money that we had paid for the corn. We have brought the money back.
We have also brought more money to buy food. We do not know who put our money back into our bags.
The manager of the house said: You must not be worried or afraid. Your God, and your father’s God gave you the money in your bags. I have the money that you paid. Then he took Simeon to them
The manager took the brothers to Joseph’s house. The manager gave them water to wash their feet, and he gave their donkeys food
Joseph’s brothers heard that they were going to eat with Joseph, so they worked until noon preparing their gifts for him.
When Joseph came home, Joseph’s brothers brought their gifts and then Joseph’s brothers bowed down before Joseph.
Joseph asked his brothers how they were and Joseph also asked: How is your father who is so old that you told me about? Is he still alive?
Joseph’s brothers answered: Our father is in good health. He is still alive. And again, Joseph Brothers bowed down before Joseph.

Then Joseph saw his brother Benjamin. (Benjamin and Joseph had the same mother.) Joseph said, Is this your youngest brother that you told me about? Then Joseph said to Benjamin, God bless you, my son!
Joseph went out of the room quickly because his heart was deeply touched over his brother. Joseph went to a private room to cry so Joseph went into his bedroom to cry.
Joseph washed his face, and he came back. Joseph was calm again and said: Put the food on the table.

Joseph’s servants seated Joseph at a table by himself. Joseph’s brothers were at another table by themselves, and the Egyptians were at a table by themselves. The Egyptians believed that it was wrong for them to eat with Hebrews
Joseph’s brothers were seated at a table facing him. The brothers were looking at each other because, to their surprise, they had been seated in order, from the oldest to the youngest.
Joseph told the manager of his house: Fill the men’s bags with wheat. Make it as full as they can carry and put each man’s money back into the top of his bag.
Joseph said, You must put my cup, the silver cup, in the bag of the youngest brother with his money for the corn.’ The manager did what Joseph told him.
Early the next morning, the brothers and their donkeys were sent back to their country.

After Joseph’s brethren had left the city, Joseph said to his manager of his house, Go and follow the men. Stop them and say to them, we were good to you! So why have you been bad to us? Why did you steal my master’s silver cup?
My master drinks from that cup, and he uses it to learn secret things. What you did was wrong!’”
The manager went out to the brothers and stopped them. The manager told them what Joseph had said
Joseph’s Brethren asked the master of the house: Why does the governor say these things? We would never do a bad thing like that.
Joseph’s Brethren said we brought back the money that we found in our bags before. We would never steal silver or gold from your master’s house.
If you find the cup in one of our bags, you may kill that person and then we will all be your slaves.
The manager said: ‘Yes, we will do as you say. But only the man in whose bag I find the cup will be my slave. All the other brothers may go.
Everyone put his bag on the ground quickly and opened it
The manager looked into the bags. He started looking in the bag of the oldest brother and stopped at the youngest brother, and then he found the cup in Benjamin’s bag
The brothers tore their clothes because they were sad and upset. They put everything they had on their donkeys and went back to the city.
When Judah and his brothers came to Joseph’s house, Joseph was still there. Judah and his brethren lay before Joseph with their faces to the ground.
Joseph asked his brethren: What have you done? You must know that a man like me can tell what will happen in the future.

Judah said to Joesph: Sir, what can we say to you? What can we say and how can we prove that we are innocent? God has shown that we are guilty. Sir, here we are now. We are your slaves, all of us, and the one who had your cup.
Joseph said: No, I would never do that. The man who had my cup will be my slave. The others may go back to your father.
Judah came to Joseph and told him: Please, sir, may I talk to you alone? Do not get angry with me. You are like Pharaoh.
Sir, when we were here the first time, you asked us if we had a father and a brother.

And we answered you, we have a father—he is an old man. And we have a younger brother. Our father loves him because he was born while our father was old. This youngest son’s brother is dead, so he is the only son who is left from that mother. Our father loves him very much
Sir, you told us to bring youngest brethren to you because you wanted to see him.
Sir, we told you That young boy cannot come. He cannot leave his father. If his father loses him, his father will be so sad that he will die
But, sir, you told us if we did not bring our youngest brother with us, then we could not see you again.
We went to our father, and we told him what you said
One day our father told us that we would have to go and buy food,
Our father told us: You know that I had only 2 children with this wife.
I let one son go away, and he was killed by a wild animal. And I haven’t seen him since.
If you take this son away from me and something happens to him, then I, an old man, will be so sad that I will die.
Now, imagine what will happen when we go home to the father without our youngest brother—he is the most important thing in our father’s life!
My father will die if he sees his son is not with us. Then it will be our fault if our father who is old dies because he is so sad.
I promised my father that I would look after his son and make sure that he was safe. I said if he did not go back to my father, then it would be my fault forever.
Sir, please let me stay here in my brother’s place. Sir, I will be your slave, but please let the boy go back with his brothers.
I cannot go back to my father if the boy is not with me. I am very afraid of what would happen to my father.
Joseph could no longer stop himself from crying. Joseph told all the other people to go away from him, and only Joseph’s brothers stayed with him. Then Joseph told his brothers who he was.
Joseph cried so loudly that the Egyptian people heard him in Pharaoh’s house.
Joseph said to his brothers: I am your brother Joseph. Is Father still alive? But Joseph’s brothers did not answer him because they were afraid.
And Joseph said to his brothers: Come here to me. I beg you, come here, and Joseph told them: I am Joseph your brother. You sold me to merchants, and they brought me here to Egypt.
Now do not feel bad. Don’t be angry with yourselves for what you did. It was God’s plan for me to come here. I am here to save people’s lives.
It is now the 2nd year that there has been a famine in the land. There will be a famine for another 5 years. There will be five more years without planting or harvest.
So God sent me here ahead of you so that I can save your people in this country. God sent me so that I can save a lot of people.
It was not you who sent me to Egypt. It was God. God chose me to tell Pharaoh what he must do. I, Joseph, am also the chief of Pharaoh’s house and the ruler of all the people in Egypt.
Go to Father immediately and tell him his son Joseph says: God has made me ruler over all of Egypt. Father, you must come to me. Do not wait.
You can stay in Goshen and be near me, you and your children and your grandchildren, your sheep, goats and cattle, and everything that belongs to you.
I will take care of you during the next five years of famine. So, you and your family will not lose everything you own.
Joseph said to his brothers: ‘You and Benjamin can see that it is I, Joseph, speaking to you.
Go and tell Father that I am an important man in Egypt. Tell him everything that you see here and hurry up. Bring Father to me.
Joseph put his arms around his brother Benjamin’s neck, and he cried. Benjamin hugged Joseph and he also cried.
Joseph kissed all his brothers, and he stood with them and they all cried. After that, his brothers talked to him.
Pharaoh learned that Joseph’s brothers had come to him. This news spread throughout Pharaoh’s house. The news was good in the sight Pharaoh and his servants were
Pharaoh said to Joseph: ‘Tell your to put on their donkeys all the food they need and go back to the land of Canaan.
Tell Joseph’s brethren to bring Joseph’s father and their families back here to me. I will give you the best land in Egypt to live on. And you Joseph family can eat the best food we have here
Joseph’s brothers must take wagons from Egypt for their children and wives. Your father must also ride on a wagon and come here.
They must not be sad about the things that they will leave in Canaan because they will get the best things here in Egypt.
Jacob’s sons did what Pharaoh said. Joseph gave them wagons as Pharaoh said and also food for their journey
Joseph gave each brother a suit of beautiful clothes. But to Benjamin he gave five suits of beautiful clothes and 300 pieces of silver

Joseph also sent gifts to his father. Joseph sent ten donkeys with bags full of many good things from Egypt. Joseph also sent ten female donkeys loaded with grain, bread, and other food for his father on his trip back.
Joseph sent his brothers back to Canaan and Joseph told his brothers: ‘Do not argue or fight on your way back
They went from Egypt, and they came to their father Jacob in Canaan.
Jospeh’s brethren told their dad that Joseph was still alive and that he was the ruler over all of Egypt. Jacob did not know what to think. At first he didn’t believe them.
Jacob’s sons told their father everything that Joseph had said. When Jacob saw the wagons that Joseph had sent him to ride on, he became very happy
And Jocob said: I am so happy. My son Joseph is still alive. I want to go and see him before I die.
Jacob left Canaan with all his people and began his trip to Egypt. First Jocob went to Beersheba. There Jacob worshiped God, the God of his father Isaac. Jacob offered sacrifices
That night Jacob dreamed that God called him and said: Jacob. And Jacob answered: Here I am.

God said: I am God, the God of your father Isaac. Go to the land of Egypt and do not be afraid. I will give you many family members in Egypt. They will become a big nation. God said: I am God, the God of your father Isaac. Go to the land of Egypt and do not be afraid. I will give you many family members in Egypt. They will become a big nation.
I will go with you to Egypt and I will bring you back here later. When you die, Joseph will close your eyes.
Jacob went away from Beersheba. Jacob sons put Jacob, their small children, and their wives in the wagons which the king of Egypt had sent.
Jacob and his family took their animals and everything that they had in Canaan. Jacob and all his family went to Egypt.
Jacob took his family to Egypt, his sons, his grandsons, his daughters and his granddaughters.
Here are the names of the Israel, Jacob’s, sons, who went to Egypt. There was Reuben, who was Jacob’s oldest son
Reuben’s sons were Hanok, Pallu, Hezron, and Karmi
Simeon’s sons were Jemuel, Jamin, Ohad, Jakin, Zohar and Shaul. Shaul’s mother was a woman from Canaan.
Levi’s sons were Gershon, Kohath and Merari.
Judah’s sons were Er, Onan, Shelah, Perez and Zerah. Er and Onan died in Canaan. Perez’s sons were Hezron and Hamul.
Issachar’s sons were Tola, Puah, Jashub and Shimron.
Zebulun’s sons were Sered, Elon and Jahleel.
These were the sons that Leah had for Jacob in Paddan-Aram. She also had a daughter. Her name was Dinah. There were 33 people in all.
Gad’s sons were Zephon, Haggi, Shuni, Ezbon, Eri, Arodi and Areli.
Asher’s sons were Imnah, Ishvah, Ishvi and Beriah. Their sister’s name was Serah. Beria’s sons were Heber and Malkiel.
These were the sons of Zilpah, the servant girl whom Laban gave to his daughter Leah. There were 16 people in all.
Jacob’s wife Rachel had 2 sons. They were Joseph and Benjamin.

Joseph married Asenath. She was the daughter of Potiphera, a priest from the town of On. She had 2 sons for Joseph in Egypt. They were Manasseh and Ephraim. Genesis 46: 21Benjamin’s sons were Bela, Beker, Ashbel, Gera, Naaman, Ehi, Rosh, Muppim, Huppim and Ard.
These were the children that Rachel had for Jacob. There were 14 people in all.
Dan had one son. His name was Hushim.
Naphtali’s sons were Jahziel, Guni, Jezer and Shillem.
These were the sons of Bilhah, the servant girl that Laban gave to his daughter Rachel. There were 7 people in all.
Of the children and grandchildren of Jacob who went to Egypt, there were 66 in all. The wives of Jacob’s sons also went with them, but they did not count how many women there were.
Joseph had another 2 sons who were born in Egypt. There were 70 people in Jacob’s family who went to Egypt.
Jacob sent Judah to go to Joseph before him, to ask him where the land of Goshen. Then they went to the land of Goshen.

Joseph told his servants to bring his chariot and Jospeh went to meet his father, Jacob in Goshen. When Joseph saw his father, Joseph put his arms around his Father and he stood and cried with him for a long time.
Jacob said to Joseph: Now I am happy, and now I can die. I have seen you again and I see that you are still alive.
Joseph said to his brothers and to his family: I will now tell Pharaoh that my brothers and my family who were in Canaan have come to me
My family are a family of shepherds. My family have always kept sheep and cattle. My family have brought all their animals and everything they own with them.
When Pharaoh calls you and asks you what kind of work you do,

My family, you must tell: Pharaoh, we are keepers of cattle from our youth until now, both we and our fathers before us, Say this in order that you may live separately and securely in the land of Goshen; for every shepherd a disgusting thing to the Egyptians.

Joseph went into Pharaoh and said, My father and my brothers and all their families are here. They have all their animals and everything they own from the land of Canaan with them. They are now in the land of Goshen
Joseph chose five of his brothers to be with him before the Pharaoh.
Pharaoh asked Joseph’s brethren: What kind of work do you do? Joseph’s brethren said: We are shepherds, and our fathers before us were also shepherds.

Joseph’s brethren asked Pharaoh: May we stay in your land as strangers? The famine is very bad in Canaan. There are no fields left with grass for our animals, so we have come to live in this land. We ask you to please let us live in Goshen.
Pharaoh said to Joseph: Your father and your brothers have come to you.

They may stay anywhere in Egypt. Choose the best part of the land for your father and your brethren. Your father and your brethren may stay in Goshen. If your brethren are good shepherds, you must tell them also to look after my sheep.
Later Joseph brought his father Jacob to meet Pharaoh.. And Jacob blessed Pharaoh.
Pharaoh asked Jacob: How old are you?
Jacob said to Pharaoh: I have had a short life with many troubles. I am only 130 years old. My father and his ancestors lived to be much older than I am.
Then Jacob blessed Pharaoh and left from his meeting with Pharaoh.
Joseph did what Pharaoh said and gave his father and brothers land in Egypt. It was the best land in Egypt, in the eastern part of the country, around Rameses
Joseph provided and supplied his father and his brothers and all his father’s household with food, according to the needs of their children.
The famine got worse; there was no food anywhere in the land. The people of Egypt and Canaan became weak with hunger.
Joseph started selling wheat to the people of Egypt and Canaan and Joseph got all the money that they had. Joseph took the money into Pharaoh’s house.

Later there was no money left in Egypt and Canaan. The people of Egypt came to Joseph and asked him: ‘Please give us food. We have no more money. Sir, if you do not give us food, we will die here in front of you.
Joseph said: If you do not have any money, give me your cattle, and I will give you food.

So the people brought their cattle to Joseph and Joseph gave them food. Joseph gave them food for their horses, sheep, goats, cattle and donkeys. And that year, Joseph gave them food and took their animals.

But the next year the people had no animals and nothing to buy food with. So they went to Joseph and said, You know that we have no money left, and all our animals belong to you. So we have nothing left, only what you see, our bodies and our land

We will die while you are watching. But if you give us food, we will give Pharaoh our land, and we will be his slaves. Give us seed so that we can plant. Then we will live and not die, and the land will grow food for us again
Joseph bought all the land in Egypt for Pharaoh. Everyone in Egypt had to sell their land because they had no food to eat. This is how all the land in Egypt became Pharaoh’s land.
And as for the people, Joseph relocated them to cities from one end of Egypt’s border to the other.
Joseph bought all the land in Egypt, but he did not buy the land of the priests, because the priest lived off the food Pharaoh given them; therefore they did not sell their land.
Joseph told the people: ‘I have bought you and your land for Pharaoh. Here is seed for you. Sow it on the ground.

But when you cut and harvest the wheat, you must give one 5th to Pharaoh and you can keep rest for yourselves. It is your seed that you must sow to get food to eat for you, your children and all the people in your families.’
They said to Joseph: ‘You have saved our lives. You were good to us and we will be Pharaoh’s slaves.
And Joseph made it a law over the land of Egypt unto this day, that Pharaoh should have the fifth part, except the land of the priests only, which became not Pharaoh’s.
Israel, Jacob, and his family lived in the land of Goshen in Egypt. They got many things and had a lot of babies, and later they became a big and powerful nation.
Jacob lived in Egypt for 17 years. Jacob life he lived was 147 years old

Jacob felt the time was coming for him to die. Jacob called his son Joseph and said to him: Will you please help me and do something for me. If you love me, put, thy hand under my thigh, and deal kindly and truly with me; bury me not, I pray thee, in Egypt:
When I die, you must take my body out of Egypt and you must bury me with my forefathers in the grave of my family.
And Jacob said, Swear unto me. And he sware unto him. Then Israel Jacob bowed in worship at the head of the bed.
Later they told Joseph that his father was sick. So Joseph went to Jacob and he took his 2 sons, Manasseh and Ephraim, with him
The people told Jacob: Your son Joseph has come to you. Jacob gathered his strength and sat up in his bed.
God told me: I will make you a great family. I will give you many children and you will be a great people. Your family will own this land forever

Joseph you have two sons. These two sons were born here in the country of Egypt before I came. Joseph two sons, Ephraim and Manasseh, will be like my own sons. They will be like Reuben and Simeon to me.

So these two boys will be my sons. They will share in everything I own. But if you have other sons, they will be your sons. But they will also be like sons to Ephraim and Manasseh—that is, in the future, they will share in everything that Ephraim and Manasseh own.
On the trip from Padan, Rachel died in the land of Canaan. We were still traveling toward Ephrath. I buried her there on the road to Ephrath.” (Ephrath is Bethlehem.)
When Jacob saw Joseph’s sons, he asked: ‘Who are they?
Joseph answered his father and said: These are the sons that God gave to me here in this land.’ Jacob said: ‘Bring them to me so that I can bless them
Jacob’s eyes were weak because he was very old, and he could not see very well. Joseph took his sons to Jacob and Jacob kissed them and held them in his arms
Then Israel said to Joseph, I never thought I would see your face again. But look! God has let me see you and your children.
Joseph took his 2 sons from Jacob’s knees and Joseph bowed with his face to the ground.

Then Joseph told his 2 sons to stand in front of Jacob. Joseph took Ephraim with his right hand to stand at Jacob’s left hand, and Joseph took Manasseh with his left hand to stand at Jacob’s right hand and he took them closer to Jacob
But Israel crossed his hands and put his right hand on the head of the younger boy Ephraim. Then he put his left hand on Manasseh, even though Manasseh was the firstborn.
Israel blessed Joseph’s sons and said: My grandfather Abraham and my father Isaac worshiped God, The God who has been my shepherd all my life to this day,

He was the Angel who saved me from all my troubles. And I pray that he will bless these boys. Now they will have my name and the name of our ancestors, Abraham and Isaac. I pray that they will grow to become great families and nations on earth.
When Joseph saw that his father put his right hand on Ephraim’s head, This didn’t make Joseph happy. Joseph took his father’s right hand and tried to put it on Manasseh’s head.
Joseph said to his father, “You have your right hand on the wrong boy. Manasseh is the firstborn. Put your right hand on him.

But his father refused and said, “I know, son. I know. Manasseh is the firstborn. He will be great and will be the father of many people. But his younger brother will be greater than he is. And the younger brother’s family will be much larger.

So Israel blessed them that day. He said, The Israelites will use your names whenever they bless someone. They will say, ‘May God make you like Ephraim and Manasseh.’” In this way Israel made Ephraim greater than Manasseh.
Jacob said to Joseph: ‘I am going to die, but God will be with you and He will bring you back to the land of your forefathers.
I now give you more than your brothers. I gave you the land that I won from the Amorites. I used my sword and bow to take that land.
Jacob called his sons and said: Come to me so that I can tell you what will happen to you later.
Come to me and listen, sons of Jacob. Listen to your father Israel.
Reuben, you were my first child. You were born when I was young and strong. You were the most honored and powerful of all my sons.

But your passion was like a flood you couldn’t control. So, you will not remain my most honored son. You climbed into your father’s bed and slept with one of his wives. You brought shame to my bed, to the bed you lay on.

Simeon and Levi are brothers. their swords are weapons of violence. Genesis 49.6 I will not join Simeon and Levi secret meetings. I will not take part in their evil plans. They have killed people out of anger and crippled animals for fun.
A curse on Simeon and Levi anger, for it is strong; a curse on their wrath, for it is cruel. Simeon and Levi will not have their own land in Israel. They will live among other people in the land.
Judah, your brothers will praise you. You will defeat your enemies. Your brothers will bow down to you.
My son, Judah, you are like a young lion standing over the animal it killed. My son, Judah, Like a lion that has lied down to rest, no one is brave enough to disturb him
Men from Judah’s family will be kings. The sign that his family rules will not leave his family before the true King, Jesus comes. Then many people will obey and serve
The true King, Jesus will tie his donkeys to the best grapevines. The true King, Jesus washes his clothes in the best wine.
The true King, Jesus eyes will be dark as red wine, and his teeth whiter than milk.
Zebulun will live near the sea. Zebulun seacoast will be a safe place for ships. Zebulun land will continue as far as the city of Sidon.
Issachar is like a strong donkey resting between two burdens
Issachar will see the land is pleasant and his resting place is good. But Issachar bowed his shoulder to bear a burden; became a band of slaves.
Dan will make sure that people do the right things, because they are also one of the tribes of Israel.
Dan is like a snake on a path. Dan is like a dangerous snake lying near the path. That snake bites a horse’s foot, and the rider falls to the ground.
Lord, I am waiting for your salvation.
A group of robbers will attack Gad, but Gad will chase them away.
Asher’s land will grow much good food. Asher will have food fit for a king!
Naphtali is like a deer that runs free in the field. Naphtali uses beautiful words.
Joseph is like a fruitful branch of a tree. A fruitful tree by a spring; Its branches run over the wall.
People attacked him and made his life hard for him. Men with arrows became his enemies.
But Joseph won the fight with his mighty bow and his skillful arms. Joseph gets his power from the Mighty God of Jacob, from the Shepherd, the Rock of Israel,

Joseph, the God of your father will help you. God Almighty will bless you, Joseph. God will bless you with rain from heaven and with water from deep in the ground. God will bless the mothers, and they will have many children and milk for all their babies.

My son, Joseph, I bless you more than my other children. This blessing will not stop as long as there are mountains and hills. The Lord will be with Joseph because he will be the leader of his brothers.
Benjamin is like a wolf that kills other animals. In the morning, he kills and eats. In the evening, he shares what is left.
These are the 12 tribes of Israel, and this is what their father, Jacob said to them. Jocob blessed his children and gave each tribe its own blessing.
Jacob told his sons: When I die, you must bury me with my fathers in the cave on the land that belonged to Ephron, the Hittite

The cave in the field of Machpelah, near Mamre in Canaan. That is the land that Abraham bought from Ephron, the Hittite, to have as a grave Genesis 49: 31 Abraham’s family buried Abraham there with his wife Sarah, and Isaac and his wives Rebekah and Leah.
Abraham bought the field and the cave on it from the Hittites
After Jacob finished talking to his sons, he lay down, put his feet back on the bed, and died.
